title: Mortality predictors in patients with suspected sepsis in the emergency department of a tertiary care hospital: a retrospective cohort study

sample_size.source_quote: The total of patients included in the study was 665 and the average age of the sample was 73 ± 19 years.

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The average age in the sample was 73.36 years old and 75% of the patients were elderly (≥ 65 years).

antibiotic_timing_hours.source_quote: When the patients were recognized by the protocol as potentially septic, the mean time of ATB start was 37.43 min.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Positive blood cultures (RR, 1.63;95% CI, 1.10 to 2.41) and administration of vasopressors in the ED (RR, 2.14;95% CI, 1.44 to 3.17) featured as independent factors of 30-day mortality in the multivariate analysis.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Administration of vasopressors at the ED
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Positive blood cultures (RR, 1.63;95% CI, 1.10 to 2.41) and administration of vasopressors in the ED (RR, 2.14;95% CI, 1.44 to 3.17) featured as independent factors of 30-day mortality in the multivariate analysis.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Administration of vasopressors at the ED
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Administration of vasopressors at the ED (RR, 2.25; CI 95%, 1.58 to 3.21) was the only variable that was considered an independent risk factor in the non-survival group in the multivariate analysis.

The highest AUROC was found for qSOFA (AUC = 0.58), and the lowest AUROC was found for the severity criteria (AUC = 0.52). A qSOFA score of two or more points was used as the reference baseline, while one or more points were used as the reference baseline for the severity criteria curve. The sensitivity for the qSOFA curve was 20% (95% CI 16%-24%), with a specificity of 87% (95% CI 82%-91%).
The sensitivity for the severity criteria curve was 18% (95% CI 15%-22%), with a specificity of 85% (95% CI 80%-89%).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Scoring any point on qSOFA was considered as an independent factor for ICU admission (qSOFA = 1 point, p = 0.02; qSOFA = 2 points, p = 0.03, and qSOFA = 3 points, p = 0.04) in the multivariate analysis.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Scoring any point on qSOFA was considered as an independent factor for ICU admission (qSOFA = 1 point, p = 0.02; qSOFA = 2 points, p = 0.03, and qSOFA = 3 points, p = 0.04) in the multivariate analysis.

mortality_predictors.source_quote: Scoring any point on qSOFA was considered as an independent factor for ICU admission (qSOFA = 1 point, p = 0.02; qSOFA = 2 points, p = 0.03, and qSOFA = 3 points, p = 0.04) in the multivariate analysis.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Positive blood cultures (RR, 1.17; CI 95%, 1.01 to 1.36) and administration of vasopressors in the ED (RR, 2.50; CI 95%, 2.20 to 2.84) were also considered as risk factors when included in the model.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Administration of vasopressors at the ED
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Positive blood cultures (RR, 1.17; CI 95%, 1.01 to 1.36) and administration of vasopressors in the ED (RR, 2.50; CI 95%, 2.20 to 2.84) were also considered as risk factors when included in the model.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The feature age ≥ 65 years (p < 0.001; RR, 1.52;95% CI, 1.19–1.93) was significantly associated with longer periods of stay in hospital along with administration of vasopressors in the ED (RR, 1.31;95% CI, 1.10 to 1.56) in the multivariate analysis.
